text.title: agents-safety-guardrails Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for agents-safety-guardrails as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Giving agents write access to production databases without an explicit rollback plan and approval gate.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent writes to production without approval can corrupt data at speed. Even a "safe" INSERT can cascade through triggers. Always require approval and have a rollback script ready before any agent touches production.

failure-mode.description: Agents calling themselves recursively via a tool without a depth counter guard.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Recursive self-calls are possible and catastrophic. A tool that spawns a sub-agent that spawns another can consume tokens exponentially. Guard with a depth counter passed through tool context; raise at depth >= 3.
